question.text: Ownership disclosure threshold for research analysts is:

answer.text: When analyst or associates own 5% or more of subject company
When analyst, entity, or relatives have actual/beneficial ownership of 1% or more securities of subject company at month-end preceding publication
When analyst owns any shares at all

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 19(i)(b): Disclose if analyst/entity/associates/relatives own 1% or more of subject company at end of month preceding publication. **The Trap:** The threshold is 1%, not 5% or 10%. And it's at month-end preceding publication date. **Cross-Exam Context:** Ch 14 NISM XV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This ownership disclosure helps readers assess potential bias. Even small ownership stakes can influence research objectivity.
